response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, testing, and open-source credentials, but the resume shows no exposure to the core domain of this role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ tooling work, which falls well short of principal-level performance architecture for deep-learning hardware.
strengths: Long, continuous engineering career (~15+ years) exceeding the years-of-experience requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience anywhere in the resume
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD performance optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload expertise
